758	\begin{definition}
759	
760	\leanok
761	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
762	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
763	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
764	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
765	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
766	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
767	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
768	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
769	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
770	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
771	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
772	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
773	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
774	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
775	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
776	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
777	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
778	  \[
779	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
780	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
781	  \]
782	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
783	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
784	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
785	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
786	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
787	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
788	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
789	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
790	
791	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
792	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
793	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
794	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
795	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
796	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
797	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
798	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
799	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
800	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
801	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
802	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
803	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
804	  free sheaves'' primitive.
805	\end{definition}
806	
807	\begin{lemma}\leanok
808	[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
809	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
810	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
811	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
812	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
813	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
814	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
815	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
816	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
817	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
818	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
819	  identity:
820	  \[
821	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
822	  \]
823	\end{lemma}
824	
825	\begin{proof}
826	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
827	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
828	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
829	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
830	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
831	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
832	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
833	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
834	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
835	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
836	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
837	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
838	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
839	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
840	\end{proof}
841	
842	\begin{lemma}\leanok
843	[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
844	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
845	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
846	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
847	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
848	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
849	\end{lemma}
850	
851	\begin{proof}
852	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
853	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
854	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
855	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
856	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
857	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
858	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
859	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
860	  \[
861	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
862	  \]
863	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
864	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
865	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
866	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
867	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
868	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
869	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
870	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
871	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
872	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
873	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
874	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
875	\end{proof}

877	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
878	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
879	
880	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
881	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
882	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
883	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
884	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
885	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
886	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
887	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
888	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
889	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
890	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
891	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.
892	
893	\begin{lemma}
894	
895	\leanok
896	[Self-minor Cramer inverse is the identity]
897	  \label{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_self}
898	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv_self}
899	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
900	  The Cramer inverse of the self-minor is the identity matrix:
901	  \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d \times d}\).
902	\end{lemma}
903	\begin{proof}
904	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
905	  The self-minor \(X^I_I\) is the identity block
906	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), and \(1^{-1} = 1\).
907	\end{proof}

982	\begin{lemma}\leanok
983	[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]

989	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
990	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
991	  multiplicative cocycle identity
992	  \[
993	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
994	  \]
995	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
996	\end{lemma}
997	\begin{proof}
998	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
999	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
1000	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
1001	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
1002	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
1003	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
1004	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
1005	  \[
1006	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
1007	  \]
1008	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
1009	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
1010	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
1011	  \[
1012	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
1013	  \]
1014	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1015	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1016	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1017	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1018	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1019	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1020	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1021	\end{proof}

1023	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1024	[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1025	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
1026	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
1027	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1028	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1029	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1030	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1031	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1032	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1033	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1034	  base-changed function:
1035	  \[
1036	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1037	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1038	  \]
1039	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1040	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1041	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1042	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1043	\end{lemma}
1044	\begin{proof}
1045	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1046	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1047	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1048	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1049	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1050	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1051	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1052	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1053	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1054	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1055	  \(q\).
1056	\end{proof}

1058	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1059	[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1060	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1061	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
1062	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1063	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1064	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1065	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
1066	  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
1067	  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp}\), and let
1068	  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
1069	  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the rank-\(d\) index set. Then the
1070	  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
1071	  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1072	  \[
1073	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
1074	      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
1075	  \]
1076	\end{lemma}
1077	\begin{proof}
1078	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1079	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1080	  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
1081	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
1082	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
1083	  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
1084	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
1085	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
1086	  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
1087	  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
1088	  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
1089	  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
1090	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
1091	  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
1092	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
1093	\end{proof}

1095	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1096	[Affine global-sections comorphism is the inducing ring homomorphism]
1097	  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1098	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1099	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme}
1100	  Let \(\varphi : A \to B\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and
1101	  \(\Spec\varphi : \Spec B \to \Spec A\) the induced morphism of affine schemes. Under the
1102	  natural identification of the global sections of an affine scheme with its coordinate ring
1103	  -- the counit \(\Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \xrightarrow{\sim} A\) of the
1104	  \(\Gamma \dashv \Spec\) adjunction, and likewise for \(B\) -- the global-sections base-change
1105	  comorphism
1106	  \[
1107	    (\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp} : \Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\Spec B, \mathcal O)
1108	  \]
1109	  is identified with \(\varphi\) itself; that is, the square relating
1110	  \((\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp}\) to \(\varphi\) through the two counit isomorphisms commutes.
1111	\end{lemma}
1112	\begin{proof}
1113	  This is the naturality of the counit isomorphism \(\Gamma \circ \Spec \cong \mathrm{id}\) of
1114	  the global-sections / spectrum adjunction: for any ring homomorphism \(\varphi\) the comorphism
1115	  of \(\Spec\varphi\) on global sections is, after transport through the natural counit
1116	  isomorphisms at \(A\) and \(B\), the homomorphism \(\varphi\). No Grassmannian-specific data
1117	  enters; the statement is the affine-scheme naturality square applied to a single ring map.
1118	\end{proof}

1308	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
1309	  \[
1310	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1311	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
1312	  \]
1313	  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
1314	  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
1315	  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
1316	\end{lemma}
1317	\begin{proof}
1318	  \uses{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1319	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1320	  The statement decomposes into three independent steps. First, the matrix-algebra core
1321	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} establishes the Cramer-inverse cocycle
1322	  \((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the triple overlap. Second, the linkage
1323	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} records that composition of matrix automorphisms realises
1324	  matrix multiplication. Third, the transport step \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1325	  carries the three transitions to the common overlap \(V_{IJK}\), aligns their source,
1326	  middle, and target sheaves through the base-change transport and the endpoint bridges, and
1327	  -- feeding it the matrix identity through the linkage -- yields exactly the stated equality
1328	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} = \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
1329	\end{proof}
1330	
1331	\begin{definition}
1332	
1333	\leanok
1334	[The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\)]
1335	  \label{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}
1336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalQuotient}
1337	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_transition,
1338	    lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:scheme_modules_glue,
1339	    def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1340	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul,
1341	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1342	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1343	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1344	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L903-L910).
1345	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1346	  % "Compatible with the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$
1347	  %  for gluing the affine pieces $U^I$, we give gluing data
1348	  %  $(g_{I,J})$ for gluing together the trivial bundles $\oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
1349	  %  by putting
1350	  %  $$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I_J)$$"
1351	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1352	  Over each overlap \(U^I_J = \Spec R^I_J\) define the bundle-transition matrix
1353	  \[
1354	    g_{I,J} \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} \;\in\; \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J),
1355	  \]
1356	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
1357	  which is invertible because \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\). The matrices
1358	  \((g_{I,J})\) are compatible with the chart-gluing cocycle \((\theta_{I,J})\)
1359	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}): over a triple overlap they
1360	  satisfy \(g_{I,K} = g_{J,K}\, g_{I,J}\) and \(g_{I,I} = 1\), the multiplicative
1361	  cocycle condition for a vector bundle. Gluing the free rank-\(d\) sheaves
1362	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) along the isomorphisms \(g_{I,J}\) over the cover
1363	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) therefore
1364	  produces a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-module
1365	  \(\mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). Its determinant line bundle \(\det \mathcal{U}\)
1366	  has transition functions \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J\), the data underlying the
1367	  Pl\"ucker embedding.
1368	\end{definition}
1369	
1370	The tautological quotient \(u\) is assembled from the chart quotients \(u^I\) by the
1371	section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}; its one remaining content is the
1372	\emph{overlap-compatibility} of the family \((u^I)\) with the bundle gluing
1373	\((g_{I,J})\). The next four blocks isolate that compatibility: a rectangular analogue of
1374	the square matrix-endomorphism API (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd},
1375	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}) for the
1376	\(r \to d\) chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_matrixEndRect},
1377	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}), the
1378	adjunction transposition that turns the descent condition into a pullback-level identity
1379	(\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}), and the matrix-core assembly
1380	that closes it (\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}).
1381	
1382	\subsection{Matrix-calculus toolbox}
1383	
1384	The chart-cover lemma (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}) and the overlap compatibility of
1385	\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient} both rest on a small calculus of \emph{rectangular} matrix
1386	homomorphisms of free sheaves, generalising the square API
1387	(\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}). The
1388	blocks below collect that toolbox: the bridge to the square case, the identity and
1389	composition laws, injectivity of the matrix presentation, the column-restriction law, the
1390	affine splitting of epimorphisms of free sheaves and the resulting matrix right inverse, the
1391	field-level minor extraction, and the presented-matrix machinery that packages a quotient as
1392	a matrix along a morphism and proves Nitsure's change-of-basis identity. They are
1393	project-bespoke infrastructure.
1394	
1395	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1396	[Square and rectangular matrix homomorphisms coincide]
1397	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1398	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1399	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1400	  For a square matrix \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), the
1401	  square and rectangular matrix homomorphisms agree:
1402	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\).
1403	\end{lemma}
1404	\begin{proof}
1405	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1406	  Both are the morphism assembled from the same biproduct components
1407	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\) over the index set \(\{1,\dots,d\}\); the two
1408	  constructions are literally the same, so the equality holds by definition.
1409	\end{proof}
1410	
1411	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1412	[Identity law for \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\)]
1413	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1414	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_one}
1415	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one, lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1416	  The rectangular homomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism:
1417	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
1418	\end{lemma}
1419	\begin{proof}
1420	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one, lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1421	  By \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect} the left side equals \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1)\),
1422	  which is the identity by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
1423	\end{proof}
1424	
1425	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1426	[Fully rectangular composition law]
1427	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}
1428	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp_rect}
1429	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum,
1430	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_add, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1431	  For \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{e\times n}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1432	  \(B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times e}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), composing two
1433	  rectangular homomorphisms realises the matrix product, the order reversed by the
1434	  contravariant component indexing exactly as in \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}: the
1435	  composite
1436	  \[
1437	    \mathcal{O}_S^{\,n} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(A)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,e}
1438	      \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1439	  \]
1440	  equals \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B \cdot A)\) (the \(d \times n\) product).
1441	\end{lemma}
1442	\begin{proof}
1443	  \uses{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
1444	  The same biproduct-extensionality computation as \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}, with the
1445	  middle biproduct indexed by \(\{1,\dots,e\}\). The \((k,\ell)\)-component of the composite
1446	  is \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(B_{k,m})\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(A_{m,\ell})\); each composite
1447	  is the scalar endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum
1448	  of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum},
1449	  \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}), so the component is
1450	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((B\cdot A)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1451	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B\cdot A)\).
1452	\end{proof}
1453	
1454	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1455	[Uniqueness of the presenting matrix]
1456	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1457	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_injective}
1458	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd}
1459	  The matrix presentation is injective: if
1460	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)\) for
1461	  \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), then \(M = N\).
1462	\end{lemma}
1463	\begin{proof}
1464	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1465	  Extract the \((p,j)\)-entry by pre-composing with the free-basis inclusion of the
1466	  \(j\)-th summand and post-composing with the projection onto the \(p\)-th summand: this
1467	  isolates the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,j})\) from
1468	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\). Equal homomorphisms therefore give
1469	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,j}) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{p,j})\); and \(a \mapsto
1470	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is injective (its inverse reads off the underlying section), whence
1471	  \(M_{p,j} = N_{p,j}\) for all \(p, j\).
1472	\end{proof}
1473	
1474	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1475	[Column restriction against the index inclusion]
1476	  \label{lem:gr_freeMap_matrixEndRect}
1477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.freeMap_matrixEndRect}
1478	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1479	  For an index map \(g : \{1,\dots,e\} \to \{1,\dots,r\}\) and
1480	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), pre-composing the
1481	  rectangular homomorphism with the free index inclusion
1482	  \(\mathrm{freeMap}(g) : \mathcal{O}_S^{\,e} \to \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) restricts to the
1483	  \(g\)-columns:
1484	  \[
1485	    \mathrm{freeMap}(g) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)
1486	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl(M_{\bullet,\, g}\bigr),
1487	  \]
1488	  where \(M_{\bullet,\, g}\) is the \(d \times e\) submatrix with \(\ell\)-th column the
1489	  \(g(\ell)\)-th column of \(M\).
1490	\end{lemma}
1491	\begin{proof}
1492	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1493	  Both sides agree on each free generator \(j\): the inclusion sends it to the \(g(j)\)-th
1494	  generator, and reading off the \((p,j)\)-entry on either side gives \(M_{p, g(j)}\). Cofan
1495	  extensionality over the \(e\) generators concludes.
1496	\end{proof}
1497	
1498	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1499	[Epimorphisms of free sheaves split over an affine]
1500	  \label{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec}
1501	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_section_of_epi_free_spec}
1502	  On an affine base \(\Spec R\), every epimorphism
1503	  \(\psi : \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,d}\) of
1504	  free sheaves splits: there is a section
1505	  \(\Phi : \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,r}\) with
1506	  \(\psi \circ \Phi = \mathrm{id}\).
1507	\end{lemma}
1508	\begin{proof}
1509	  Under the fully faithful additive functor \(\mathrm{Mod}_R \to (\Spec R)\text{-Mod}\)
1510	  taking a module to its associated quasi-coherent sheaf, the free sheaves correspond to the
1511	  free modules \(R^{\,r}, R^{\,d}\) and \(\psi\) to a surjection of modules. A free module is
1512	  projective, so this surjection admits a module-level section; transporting that section
1513	  back through full faithfulness produces the required \(\Phi\).
1514	\end{proof}
1515	
1516	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1517	[Matrix right inverse over \(\Spec R\)]
1518	  \label{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}
1519	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}
1520	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1521	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1522	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(\Spec R, \mathcal{O}))\) with
1523	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) an epimorphism, the matrix admits a right inverse: there is
1524	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(\Gamma(\Spec R, \mathcal{O}))\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1525	\end{lemma}
1526	\begin{proof}
1527	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1528	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1529	  Split the epimorphism by \cref{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec} and read its section
1530	  off as a matrix \(G\), so that \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(G)\) is the section. The composition
1531	  law (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}) gives \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M\cdot G)\) as the
1532	  composite of section and epimorphism, which is the identity; with
1533	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}) and injectivity
1534	  of the presentation (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}) this forces \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1535	\end{proof}
1536	
1537	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1538	[Matrix right inverse over an affine scheme]
1539	  \label{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect}
1540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect}
1541	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1542	  For an affine scheme \(S\) and
1543	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) with
1544	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) an epimorphism, there is
1545	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1546	\end{lemma}
1547	\begin{proof}
1548	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1549	  Transport along the canonical isomorphism \(S \cong \Spec\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\). The
1550	  base-change naturality of \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback})
1551	  identifies the pulled-back homomorphism, through the free-pullback comparisons, with
1552	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the transported matrix, reducing the claim to the \(\Spec\)
1553	  case (\cref{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}); pulling the resulting
1554	  right inverse back along the (invertible) global-sections map gives \(G\) over \(S\).
1555	\end{proof}

1557	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1558	[Field-level minor extraction]
1559	  \label{lem:gr_exists_isUnit_submatrix}
1560	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_isUnit_submatrix}
1561	  Let \(K\) be a field, \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(K)\) and
1562	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(K)\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\). Then some size-\(d\)
1563	  column-index set \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) gives an invertible minor: the \(d \times d\)
1564	  submatrix \(M_I\) of the \(I\)-columns (in their natural order) is a unit.
1565	\end{lemma}
1566	\begin{proof}
1567	  The right inverse makes the column map \(K^{\,r} \to K^{\,d}\) surjective, so the columns of
1568	  \(M\) span \(K^{\,d}\). A spanning family contains a basis; choosing \(d\) columns forming a
1569	  basis, the corresponding \(d \times d\) submatrix has linearly independent columns and is
1570	  therefore invertible.
1571	\end{proof}

1573	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1574	[Inverse comparison intertwines the index inclusion]
1575	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
1576	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
1577	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1578	  For a morphism \(p : W \to V\) and an index map \(g : \{1,\dots,n\} \to \{1,\dots,m\}\), the
1579	  inverse free-pullback comparisons \(Q_n, Q_m\) (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) intertwine the
1580	  index inclusions:
1581	  \[
1582	    Q_n^{-1} \,\circ\, p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{freeMap}(g)\bigr)
1583	      \;=\; \mathrm{freeMap}(g) \,\circ\, Q_m^{-1}.
1584	  \]
1585	\end{lemma}
1586	\begin{proof}
1587	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1588	  This is the inverse form of the forward naturality of \(\mathrm{freeMap}\) under pullback:
1589	  cancel the comparison isomorphisms \(Q_n, Q_m\) on both sides and apply the forward
1590	  intertwining identity \(p^{*}(\mathrm{freeMap}(g))\circ Q_m = Q_n \circ \mathrm{freeMap}(g)\).
1591	\end{proof}

1593	\begin{definition}\leanok
1594	[Presented matrix of a quotient along a morphism]
1595	  \label{def:gr_presentedMatrix}
1596	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix}
1597	  \uses{def:gr_rankQuotient, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1598	  Let \(x = \langle\mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1599	  (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), \(j : P \to T\) a morphism, and \(I\) a size-\(d\) subset such
1600	  that the pulled-back chart composite \(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\) is an isomorphism. The
1601	  \emph{presented matrix} \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I) \in
1602	  \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(P, \mathcal{O}_P))\) is the matrix that reads off,
1603	  entrywise via the section-extraction \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\), the conjugated free-sheaf
1604	  homomorphism
1605	  \[
1606	    Q_r^{-1} \,\circ\, j^{*}q \,\circ\, \bigl(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\bigr)^{-1}
1607	      \,\circ\, Q_d
1608	      \;:\; \mathcal{O}_P^{\,r} \longrightarrow \mathcal{O}_P^{\,d},
1609	  \]
1610	  where \(Q_r, Q_d\) are the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}). It
1611	  generalises the chart matrix (the case where \(j\) is the inclusion of the chart locus
1612	  \(T_I\)) and is the vehicle of the overlap comparison.
1613	\end{definition}

1633	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1634	[Minor of the presented matrix presents the \(J\)-chart composite]
1635	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
1636	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}

1657	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1658	[Change of basis between chart presentations]
1659	  \label{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}
1660	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}
1661	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix,
1662	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1663	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1664	  Along a morphism \(j\) on which both the \(I\)- and \(J\)-chart composites pull back to
1665	  isomorphisms, the \(I\)-presented matrix is the product of its own \(J\)-minor with the
1666	  \(J\)-presented matrix -- Nitsure's overlap identity \(M^I = M^I_J \cdot M^J\):
1667	  \[
1668	    \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)
1669	      \;=\; \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)_{\bullet,\, J}
1670	        \,\cdot\, \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, J).
1671	  \]
1672	\end{lemma}
1673	\begin{proof}
1674	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor,
1675	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1676	  Apply \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) and use injectivity (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}).
1677	  By the composition law (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}) the right side becomes the
1678	  composite of the two presented homomorphisms; substituting
1679	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix} (for both factors) and
1680	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor} (for the minor), the pulled-back
1681	  \(J\)-chart composite \(j^{*}(s_J \circ q)\) cancels with its inverse, leaving
1682	  \(Q_r^{-1} \circ j^{*}q \circ (j^{*}(s_I \circ q))^{-1} \circ Q_d =
1683	  \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I))\).
1684	\end{proof}
1685	
1686	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1687	[Invertible presentation forces a unit matrix]
1688	  \label{lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect}
1689	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect}
1690	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1691	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1692	  Converse of \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}: if \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)\) is an
1693	  isomorphism for a square \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\),
1694	  then \(N\) is a unit (an invertible matrix).
1695	\end{lemma}
1696	\begin{proof}
1697	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective,
1698	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1699	  Read off the inverse morphism by its \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\) matrix \(N'\), so that
1700	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N') = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)^{-1}\). The composition law
1701	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}), the identity law
1702	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}) and injectivity (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective})
1703	  turn the two-sided inverse relation of morphisms into \(N \cdot N' = 1 = N' \cdot N\),
1704	  exhibiting \(N\) as a unit.
1705	\end{proof}
1706	
1707	\begin{definition}
1708	
1709	\leanok
1710	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves]
1711	  \label{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1712	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect}
1713	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1714	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d, r \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1715	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a rectangular matrix
1716	  of global functions. The \emph{rectangular matrix homomorphism}
1717	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is the morphism of free sheaves
1718	  \[
1719	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \;:\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}
1720	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1721	  \]
1722	  whose \((k,\ell)\)-component -- \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\) an output index,
1723	  \(\ell \in \{1,\dots,r\}\) an input index -- under the presentation of
1724	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) and \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as finite biproducts of copies of the
1725	  unit module \(\mathbf 1\), is the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\)
1726	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is assembled from these components by the universal property
1727	  of the two biproducts, exactly as the square \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\)
1728	  (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}) and the chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). When
1729	  \(r = d\) it specialises to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\); and the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1730	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is by construction \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) of the
1731	  universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}).
1732	\end{definition}
1733	
1734	\begin{lemma}
1735	
1736	\leanok
1737	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism is natural under pullback]
1738	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1739	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_pullback}
1740	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback,
1741	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1742	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and
1743	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), with entrywise
1744	  base-changed matrix \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T,\mathcal{O}_T))\).
1745	  Write \(Q_r : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) and
1746	  \(Q_d : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) for the
1747	  free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) at the index sets
1748	  \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Then the pullback of
1749	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q_r\) and \(Q_d\), to the
1750	  rectangular homomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1751	  \[
1752	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\bigr)
1753	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q_r .
1754	  \]
1755	\end{lemma}
1756	\begin{proof}
1757	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1758	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1759	  Identical to the square case \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, with the source and target
1760	  biproducts now indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\) respectively. The
1761	  pullback functor \(p^{*}\), a left adjoint, is additive and biproduct-preserving; through
1762	  the coproduct comparisons underlying the free-pullback isomorphisms
1763	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation of
1764	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\)) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1765	  (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\)), the reassociation of the iterated pullbacks being
1766	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the pulled-back
1767	  scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the comparison-conjugate of
1768	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the two biproducts, the
1769	  conjugating one-element comparisons combine into \(Q_r\) on the source and \(Q_d\) on the
1770	  target, giving \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)) = Q_d^{-1}\circ
1771	  \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q_r\).
1772	\end{proof}
1773	
1774	\begin{lemma}
1775	
1776	\leanok
1777	[Square-after-rectangular composition law]
1778	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1779	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp}
1780	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1781	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1782	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1783	  \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), post-composing the
1784	  rectangular homomorphism with a square one realises the matrix product, with the order
1785	  reversed by the contravariance of the component indexing exactly as in
1786	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}:
1787	  \[
1788	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
1789	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N \cdot M),
1790	  \]
1791	  the composite \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)}
1792	  \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) (here
1793	  \(N \cdot M\) is the \(d \times r\) product).
1794	\end{lemma}
1795	\begin{proof}
1796	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1797	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1798	  The same biproduct-extensionality computation as \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}, the only
1799	  change being that the leftmost (input) biproduct is indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) rather
1800	  than \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). The composite of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again
1801	  biproduct-assembled, with \((k,\ell)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(m\) of the
1802	  composites of components, \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{k,m}) \circ
1803	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{m,\ell})\). Each composite of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar
1804	  endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum of scalar
1805	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence
1806	  that component is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(\sum_m N_{k,m} M_{m,\ell}) =
1807	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((N\cdot M)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1808	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N\cdot M)\).
1809	\end{proof}
1810	
1811	\begin{lemma}
1812	
1813	\leanok
1814	[Adjunction transpose of the chart-overlap condition]
1815	  \label{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}
1816	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}
1817	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_bundleTransition, def:gr_modules_glueHom,
1818	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app,
1819	    lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1820	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1821	  Fix size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\) and let \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\),
1822	  \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be the overlap immersion and transition
1823	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The per-chart components \(u^I\) of the tautological quotient
1824	  are the adjoint transposes, along the chart immersions, of the chart quotients
1825	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient} (precomposed with the free-pullback comparison). Their
1826	  descent compatibility -- the hypothesis the gluing primitive \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
1827	  consumes, namely that the bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition})
1828	  intertwines the pullbacks of \(u^I\) and \(u^J\) over \(U^I_J\) -- is equivalent, under the
1829	  pullback--pushforward adjunction of the overlap immersion, to the pullback-level identity
1830	  of morphisms \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\)
1831	  \[
1832	    g_{I,J} \,\circ\, f_{IJ}^{*}\,u^I
1833	      \;=\; (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\,u^J,
1834	  \]
1835	  understood through the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) on
1836	  source and target.
1837	\end{lemma}
1838	\begin{proof}
1839	  \uses{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app, lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib,
1840	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1841	  The components are produced by applying the adjunction hom-equivalence to the chart
1842	  quotients; the descent condition of \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} is stated on those
1843	  transposes. Transposing back across the adjunction is the naturality of the conjugate
1844	  (mate) construction against the hom-equivalences, \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app},
1845	  together with the compatibility of the conjugate with the iterated-pullback comparison
1846	  \cref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} reassociating the two pullbacks
1847	  \(f_{IJ}^{*}\) and \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*}\) via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The
1848	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} re-present both sides as morphisms
1849	  of the trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\), under
1850	  which the transposed condition becomes the displayed pullback-level identity.
1851	\end{proof}
1852	
1853	\begin{lemma}
1854	
1855	\leanok
1856	[Overlap compatibility of the tautological quotient]

1	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianCells
2	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
3	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
4	
5	/-!
6	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
7	
8	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
9	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
10	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
11	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
12	
13	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
21	
22	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
23	
24	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
25	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
26	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
27	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
28	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
29	
30	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
31	sheaves" primitive. -/
32	
33	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
36	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
37	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
38	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
39	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
40	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
41	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
42	    (by
43	      intro Y Z f
44	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
45	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
46	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
47	      congr 1)
48	
49	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
50	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
51	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
52	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
53	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
54	
55	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
56	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
57	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
58	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
59	  ext Y
60	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
61	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
62	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
63	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
64	  exact map_one _
65	
66	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
67	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
68	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
69	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
70	  ext Y
71	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
72	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
73	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
74	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
75	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
76	  rfl
77	
78	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
79	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
80	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
82	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
83	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
87	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
88	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
89	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
90	
91	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
92	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
93	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
94	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
95	  exact one_smul _ _
96	
97	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
98	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
99	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
100	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
101	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
102	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
103	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
104	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
105	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
106	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
107	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
108	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
109	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
110	
111	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
112	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
113	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
114	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
115	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
116	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
117	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
118	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
119	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
121	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
122	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
123	
124	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
125	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
126	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
127	  classical
128	  induction s using Finset.induction with
129	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
130	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
131	
132	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
133	
134	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
135	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
136	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
137	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
138	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
139	
140	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
141	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
142	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
143	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
144	
145	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
146	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
147	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
148	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
149	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
150	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
151	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
152	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
153	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
154	
155	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
156	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
157	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
158	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
159	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
160	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
161	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
162	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
163	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
164	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
165	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
166	
167	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
168	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
169	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
170	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
171	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
172	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
173	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
174	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
175	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
176	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
177	    congr 1
178	    funext i q
179	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
180	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
181	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
182	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
183	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
184	
185	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
186	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
187	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
188	  rw [matrixEnd]
189	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
190	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
191	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
192	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
193	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
194	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
195	    by_cases h : p = i
196	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
197	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
198	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]
199

1858	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1859	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,

1862	    lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,

1864	  For every pair of size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\), the chart quotients \(u^I\)
1865	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) satisfy the overlap-compatibility condition required by the
1866	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} for the bundle gluing \((g_{I,J})\):
1867	  the pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\)
1868	  of \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} holds on each overlap \(U^I_J\).
1869	\end{lemma}
1870	\begin{proof}
1871	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1872	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1873	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq,
1874	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1875	  By \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} it suffices to prove the
1876	  pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\) on
1877	  \(U^I_J\). Both sides are morphisms of trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to
1878	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); write each as \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of a \(d \times r\)
1879	  matrix over the overlap ring \(R^I_J\) and compare matrices.
1880	
1881	  Since \(u^I = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
1882	  base-change naturality \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} identifies \(f_{IJ}^{*} u^I\),
1883	  through the free-pullback comparisons, with \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the localised
1884	  matrix \(X^I\) over \(R^I_J\) (the global-sections base change is the away-localisation
1885	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}). The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) is
1886	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)
1887	  (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}); hence by the
1888	  square-after-rectangular composition law \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp},
1889	  \[
1890	    g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I
1891	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot X^I\bigr).
1892	  \]
1893	  The transition image matrix identity \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
1894	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}, transported across the overlap by
1895	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}) turns the right-hand matrix into \(X^J\). Symmetrically,
1896	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}_J\) of \(X^J\) is, by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} along
1897	  \(t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI}\), the comparison form of \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\). Therefore
1898	  the two sides agree, which is the required overlap compatibility.
1899	\end{proof}

1901	\begin{definition}
1902	
1903	\leanok
1904	[The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\)]
1905	  \label{def:tautological_quotient}

1909	    def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
1910	    def:gr_modules_glueHom, lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1911	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1912	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1913	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L908-L910).
1914	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1915	  % "This is compatible with the homomorphisms $u^I$,
1916	  %  so we get a surjective homomorphism
1917	  %  $u : \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$."
1918	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1919	  The chart quotients \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1920	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing
1921	  \((g_{I,J})\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}): over
1922	  each overlap \(U^I_J\) one has the identity of \(d \times r\) matrices
1923	  \[
1924	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,
1925	  \]
1926	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1927	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). At the
1928	  module level this overlap compatibility -- the equalising condition consumed by the
1929	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} -- is exactly
1930	  \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}, whose proof routes the matrix identity above
1931	  through the rectangular pullback/composition API
1932	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}) and the
1933	  adjunction transpose \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}.
1934	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1935	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1936	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1937	  \[
1938	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1939	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1940	  \]
1941	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1942	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1943	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1944	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1945	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1946	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.

1949	\section{The functor of points}
1950	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1951	
1952	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1953	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1954	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1955	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1956	identities that make that action functorial.
1957	
1958	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1959	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1960	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1961	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1962	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1963	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1964	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1965	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1966	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1967	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1968	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1969	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1970	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1971	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1972	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1973	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1974	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1975	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1976	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1977	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1978	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1979	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1980	  functor's value at \(T\).
1981	
1982	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1983	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1984	  \[
1985	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1986	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1987	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1988	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1989	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1990	  \]
1991	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1992	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1993	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1994	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1995	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1996	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1997	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1998	  map of equivalence classes.
1999	\end{definition}
2000	
2001	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
2002	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
2003	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
2004	
2005	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2006	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
2007	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
2008	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
2009	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
2010	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
2011	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
2012	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
2013	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
2014	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
2015	\end{lemma}
2016	
2017	\begin{proof}
2018	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
2019	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
2020	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
2021	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
2022	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
2023	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
2024	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
2025	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
2026	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
2027	\end{proof}

2050	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}

2120	\begin{definition}
2121	
2122	\leanok
2123	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
2124	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
2125	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
2126	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
2127	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
2128	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2129	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2130	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
2131	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2132	  % "represents the
2133	  %  contravariant functor
2134	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2135	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2136	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2137	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2138	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
2139	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2140	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
2141	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
2142	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
2143	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
2144	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
2145	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
2146	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
2147	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2148	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
2149	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
2150	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
2151	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
2152	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
2153	  \[
2154	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
2155	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
2156	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
2157	  \]
2158	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
2159	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
2160	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
2161	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
2162	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
2163	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
2164	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
2165	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
2166	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
2167	\end{definition}

2169	\section{The universal property}
2170	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
2171	
2172	The representability theorem \cref{thm:grassmannian_universal_property} has two halves:
2173	the forward map \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\)
2174	and its inverse, which manufactures a morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) out of a rank-\(d\)
2175	quotient \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\). The forward map needs the tautological
2176	quotient to actually be a quotient -- that \(u\) is an epimorphism
2177	(\cref{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}). The inverse is built Zariski-locally and glued: for
2178	each size-\(d\) subset \(I\) one isolates the open locus \(T_I \subseteq T\) where the
2179	\(I\)-indexed columns of \(q\) form a basis of \(\mathcal{F}\), classifies \(T_I \to U^I\)
2180	there, and glues over the cover \(\{T_I\}\). The blocks of this section supply the four
2181	ingredients of that inverse: the iso-locus of a sheaf morphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}) and
2182	the fact that a morphism becomes invertible after restriction to its iso-locus
2183	(\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}); the chart locus \(T_I\) (\cref{def:chartLocus})
2184	and the proof that the chart loci cover \(T\) (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}); and the
2185	glued classifying morphism itself (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}).
2186	
2187	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2188	[The tautological quotient is an epimorphism]
2189	  \label{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}
2190	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_epi}
2191	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
2192	  The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
2193	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}) is an epimorphism of
2194	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-modules.
2195	\end{lemma}
2196	\begin{proof}
2197	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
2198	  Being an epimorphism of sheaves of modules is local on the base: a morphism is epi
2199	  precisely when its restriction to every member of an open cover is epi, since the
2200	  cokernel is computed chartwise and a sheaf vanishing on a cover vanishes. The charts
2201	  \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and over \(U^I\) the tautological quotient,
2202	  transported through the trivialisation \(\mathcal{U}|_{U^I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
2203	  of \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}, is the chart quotient \(u^I :
2204	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}).
2205	  Each \(u^I\) is a split epimorphism: its \(I\)-minor is the identity \(1_{d\times d}\),
2206	  so the coordinate inclusion \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow
2207	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) on the \(I\)-columns is a section of \(u^I\), whence \(u^I\) is
2208	  epi (\cref{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}). As \(u^I\) is epi on every chart, \(u\) is epi.
2209	\end{proof}

2211	\begin{definition}\leanok
2212	[Iso-locus of a morphism of sheaves of modules]
2213	  \label{def:isoLocus}
2214	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isoLocus}
2215	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) a morphism of sheaves
2216	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The \emph{iso-locus} \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi) \subseteq X\)
2217	  is the open subscheme
2218	  \[
2219	    \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)
2220	      \;=\; \bigsqcup\,\bigl\{\, U \subseteq X \text{ open} \;:\;
2221	        \varphi|_U := (\iota_U^{*}\varphi) \text{ is an isomorphism} \,\bigr\},
2222	  \]
2223	  the supremum (union) of all opens \(U\) over which the pullback \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) of
2224	  \(\varphi\) to the open subscheme \(U\) is an isomorphism of sheaves of
2225	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules. Equivalently, a point \(t \in X\) lies in
2226	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) iff \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism over some open
2227	  neighbourhood of \(t\). It is the largest open on which \(\varphi\) is invertible; Mathlib
2228	  has no iso-locus primitive for morphisms of sheaves of modules, so it is set up directly
2229	  as this supremum.
2230	\end{definition}
2231	
2232	\begin{lemma}[Stalk-wise iso criterion]
2233	  \label{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2234	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso}
2235	  \mathlibok
2236	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
2237	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves on a topological space \(X\) valued in a
2238	  concrete category whose forgetful functor reflects isomorphisms and preserves limits and
2239	  filtered colimits, and let \(f : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) be a morphism. If the
2240	  induced map on stalks \(f_t : \mathcal{F}_t \to \mathcal{G}_t\) is an isomorphism for every
2241	  point \(t \in X\), then \(f\) is an isomorphism.
2242	\end{lemma}
2243	
2244	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2245	[A morphism is invertible over its iso-locus]
2246	  \label{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
2247	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
2248	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2249	  For a morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) of sheaves of
2250	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the pullback of \(\varphi\) to the iso-locus
2251	  (\cref{def:isoLocus}),
2252	  \[
2253	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\varphi
2254	      \;:\;
2255	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{M}
2256	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2257	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{N},
2258	  \]
2259	  is an isomorphism.
2260	\end{lemma}
2261	\begin{proof}
2262	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2263	  Being an isomorphism is a stalk-local property: by the stalk-wise criterion
2264	  (\cref{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}) it suffices to show that the pulled-back
2265	  morphism induces an isomorphism on the stalk at every point \(t \in
2266	  \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\). By the definition of the iso-locus as a union of opens on
2267	  which \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}), each such \(t\) has an
2268	  open neighbourhood \(U \subseteq \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) with \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) an
2269	  isomorphism; restriction of an isomorphism is an isomorphism, and pullback commutes with
2270	  taking stalks, so the stalk of the morphism at \(t\) coincides with the stalk of
2271	  \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) at \(t\), which is invertible. Hence every stalk map is an
2272	  isomorphism, and the criterion concludes that the global pullback over
2273	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism.
2274	\end{proof}
